vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inaccurate regarding current specimen presence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/lost. P2 (Mexican Mint) is absent/lost. P3 (Pothos) is present but severely diminished. P4 (Silver Guest) is absent/lost.

plant_audit.P3_Pothos: Single leaf remains, showing signs of chlorosis and dehydration. The petiole is weak.
plant_audit.Unidentified_Sprout: A single, lanceolate green leaf has emerged in the pot previously associated with P3/P4. This is a new, uncatalogued specimen.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Surface appears dry and compacted with significant debris (white fragments, likely eggshell or perlite).
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no significant biological growth outside of pots.
biome_observations.anomalies: Presence of white, calcified-looking debris in the soil of the lower pot.

temporal_deltas.T_earliest_to_T_minus_5: Initial state shows a withered brown leaf and a single green leaf in the lower pot.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_4_to_T_minus_1: Progressive loss of structural integrity in the lower pot's leaf; the brown leaf has desiccated further.
temporal_deltas.current_state: The lower pot contains a single, upright, lanceolate leaf that appears to be a new sprout or a survivor of a different species than the original Pothos.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of severe decline. Most registered specimens have suffered systemic loss. The remaining Pothos leaf is struggling, and the new sprout in the lower pot is the only sign of active, albeit fragile, biological activity.
anomalies: New Introduction: Unidentified lanceolate sprout in the lower pot.
Systemic Loss: P1, P2, and P4 are no longer visible as viable plants.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment has undergone a catastrophic reduction in diversity. The original registry is no longer representative of the physical reality. The desk now hosts only a single, struggling Pothos leaf in the upper pot (guarded by the rabbit anchor) and a solitary, unidentified sprout in the lower pot. The soil in both pots shows signs of neglect, with dry, crusty surfaces and scattered debris. The transition from the earliest image to the current state is defined by the total loss of the Mexican Mint and String of Nickels, leaving a barren landscape.
